task_completion.description: Open the SeisComP Map View configuration file for editing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., nano, vim, gedit) or terminal command modifying the file `~/.seiscomp/scmv.cfg`.

task_completion.description: Set the events.visibleTimeSpan parameter to at least 1000 days.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text `events.visibleTimeSpan = 1000d` (or a value >= 1000d) is visible in the configuration file being edited.

task_completion.description: Set the events.retention parameter to at least 1000 days.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text `events.retention = 1000d` (or a value >= 1000d) is visible in the configuration file being edited.

task_completion.description: Launch the SeisComP Map View (scmv) application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SeisComP Map View (`scmv`) GUI window is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: Navigate the map to the target event region.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The map view is centered on or clearly displays Japan, specifically the Noto Peninsula region.

task_completion.description: Display the historical earthquake event symbol on the map.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An earthquake event symbol (typically a colored circle) is visible on the map at the Noto Peninsula location.

task_completion.description: Save a screenshot of the scmv window to the required path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal command (e.g., scrot, import, gnome-screenshot) saving the screenshot to `~/scmv_noto_map.png` or `/home/ga/scmv_noto_map.png`, or a file manager/image viewer confirming the file exists at that exact path.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual SeisComP Map View application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The window showing the map has genuine SeisComP Map View UI elements (menus, toolbars, status bars) and is not just a downloaded image opened in an image viewer.

integrity.description: Verify the configuration file was actually modified and saved.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses a real text editor to save the changes or a valid shell redirection command, rather than just echoing text to the terminal without writing to the file.

integrity.description: Verify the screenshot is captured from the agent's live environment.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshot command captures the active desktop/window containing the live `scmv` application, rather than downloading an existing screenshot from the internet.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected screenshot path is exactly `/home/ga/scmv_noto_map.png`. The configuration requires both `events.visibleTimeSpan` and `events.retention` to be set to at least 1000 days (e.g., '1000d' or 86400000 seconds). The target event is the 2024 Noto Peninsula, Japan Earthquake, so the map must show Japan with an event symbol in the Noto Peninsula region.
